metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly in the plan whether `--class` is required or optional on bug close, and add an AC (or note) specifying the expected exit code when `--class` is omitted.
evidence: 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`' implies required parity
'additive/optional field (readers tolerate absence)' implies optional schema field, but is ambiguous about CLI enforcement
No AC tests `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` (without `--class`) to specify the expected behavior
findings.finding: The plan does not state whether `--class` is required or optional when closing a bug ticket. 'Replacing' the required `--reason` implies required, but 'additive/optional field' implies optional. No AC tests the omission case (close a bug without `--class`).
findings.location: Approach section ‚Äî 'Additive/optional field (readers tolerate absence); back-out = stop requiring/writing it'
scenarios: Executor implements `--class` as required (matching old `--reason` behavior) ‚Äî omission is a CLI error
Executor implements `--class` as optional ‚Äî omission silently writes no `close_class`, diverging from the intent of 'replacing' the required reason
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Approach: '`--class` is required on bug close (mirrors the old `--reason` requirement); omitting it exits nonzero with a message listing allowed values.' Add a fourth AC: `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` (no `--class`) exits nonzero.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `transition_close.py`'s `_completion_precheck` bug-close guard (line 121) to the list of sites that must be updated when replacing `bug_close_reason_ok` with the `--class` enum check.
evidence: Plan states: 'validate the enum for bug closes (replacing `bug_close_reason_ok`) and write `close_class` into `status_data`' ‚Äî only `txn.py` is named
transition_close.py:121: `if ticket_type == 'bug' and not txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason):`
txn.py:150-156: `bug_close_reason_ok` exists and is called at txn.py:228 AND transition_close.py:121
`transition_close.py` is listed in Files but its `_completion_precheck` guard is not mentioned as requiring change
findings.finding: The plan asserts `bug_close_reason_ok` is being 'replaced' and only lists `txn.py` for that change, but `bug_close_reason_ok` is called from a SECOND site in `transition_close._completion_precheck` (transition_close.py:121) that the plan does not address. If `--reason` is removed/relaxed for bug closes, that second guard will still reject closes lacking a `Fixed:`/`Escalated:` prefix, silently breaking the `--class` flow when the completion-verification gate is on.
scenarios: User runs `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --class regression` with the completion-verification gate enabled; _completion_precheck fires first (outside the lock), calls bug_close_reason_ok(''), gets False, raises CommandError ‚Äî the close is rejected before txn.py is even reached.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_completion_precheck`, replace the `txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason)` call with a check that a valid `close_class` value was supplied (parallel to what txn.py's transition_core does after the change). Alternatively, when `close_class` is set, skip the reason pre-check entirely in `_completion_precheck`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` to the Files list and specify that `process_status` must fold `close_class` from the STATUS event's `data` dict into the top-level ticket state (analogous to how `_fold_claimed_session` folds session provenance).
evidence: Plan states: 'write `close_class` into `status_data` (currently the reason is discarded)' and AC1: `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` matches

reducer/_processors.py:270-283: process_status only folds 'status', 'current_status', 'parent_status_uuid', 'last_status_env_id', 'last_reopened_at', and session fields ‚Äî no passthrough of arbitrary data keys
src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py is absent from the plan's Files list
findings.finding: The plan asserts that writing `close_class` into `status_data` means it 'survives' and will be readable via `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` (AC1). But the reducer's `process_status` in `_processors.py` does NOT automatically promote arbitrary `status_data` dict keys to the top-level ticket state: only `status`, `current_status`, `parent_status_uuid`, the session fields, and `last_reopened_at` are explicitly folded. `close_class` in the event's `data` dict will be stored on disk but will NOT appear in `reduce_ticket()` output, making AC1 impossible to pass without a reducer change.
findings.location: Approach section ('written into `status_data` so it survives') / AC1; src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:195-292
scenarios: `rebar show <bug> --output json` returns the compiled reduce_ticket() state; since close_class is never folded from the STATUS event's data dict into state, jq -e '.close_class=="regression"' returns exit 1, failing AC1.
findings.suggested_fix: In `process_status` (or a new `_fold_close_class` helper), after a `* -> closed` edge is applied, do: `if data.get('status') == 'closed' and data.get('close_class'): state['close_class'] = data['close_class']`. Also add `close_class` to `ticket_state.schema.json` as the plan already notes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reducer/_processors.py` to the Files edit-set: `process_status` must fold `close_class` from winning STATUS event `data` into `state["close_class"]` (same pattern as `_fold_claimed_session` for `claimed_session`).
evidence: AC: 'rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e \'\''`.close_class=="regression"'\'' matches'

`reducer/_processors.py:process_status` is the only place STATUS event data is folded into top-level ticket state (e.g. `_fold_claimed_session` at line 171-192 folds `data["session"]` ‚Üí `state["claimed_session"]`)
ABSENCE: `reducer/_processors.py` not listed in Files section
No existing mechanism surfaces arbitrary `status_data` keys into top-level state automatically
findings.finding: `reducer/_processors.py` is missing from the edit-set: `process_status` must fold `close_class` from `status_data` into top-level ticket state (mirroring how `_fold_claimed_session` folds `session` into `claimed_session`) for the AC assertion `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` to pass.
scenarios: Test writes `close_class=regression` into status_data via txn.py, but `rebar show` returns the reducer-computed state dict which never received the fold ‚Üí jq assertion fails ‚Üí AC 1 is not satisfiable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: In `process_status` (non-fork and fork-winner branches), after the existing `_fold_claimed_session` call, add: `if data.get('status') == 'closed' and data.get('close_class'): state['close_class'] = data['close_class']`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` to the Files list and specify a fold step (analogous to `_fold_claimed_session`) inside `process_status` that copies `close_class` from the winning STATUS event's `data` dict into `state['close_class']` on the `* ‚Üí closed` edge.
evidence: The plan's Files list does not include src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py.

process_status in _processors.py (lines 195-291) only folds status, current_status, parent_status_uuid, session, harness, remote_session from event data into state ‚Äî there is no passthrough of arbitrary extra keys.
The exactly parallel claimed_session field required an explicit _fold_claimed_session helper (txn.py lines 56-76, processors.py lines 171-192) ‚Äî the plan omits this step for close_class.
AC: `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` reads the reduced state, which will not contain close_class without a fold step.
findings.finding: The plan never adds the fold step in the reducer's process_status (or a _fold_close_class helper) that promotes close_class from the raw STATUS event's data dict into the reduced ticket state. Without this step, close_class is durable in the event file but invisible to rebar show, making the primary AC assertion (`jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` on show output) fail.
findings.location: Files section (missing: src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py); Acceptance Criteria line 1
scenarios: Implementer writes close_class into status_data correctly but omits the reducer fold; all CLI tests asserting rebar show output fail because the field is absent from the reduced state.
The field is only visible by reading the raw STATUS event JSON directly, not through any public surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `_fold_close_class(state, data)` helper that sets `state['close_class'] = data.get('close_class')` when `data.get('status') == 'closed'`, and call it from `process_status` in both the fork-winner and normal-update branches (mirroring `_fold_claimed_session`). List `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` in Files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the Files entry for `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`, explicitly note that the `_completion_precheck` guard on line 121 (`bug_close_reason_ok(reason)`) must also be replaced/updated to accept a valid `close_class` in lieu of a `--reason`, so both call sites are replaced in lockstep.
evidence: transition_close.py line 121: `if ticket_type == 'bug' and not txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason):` raises CommandError with the old Fixed:/Escalated: message.
txn.py line 150-156: bug_close_reason_ok returns False for an empty reason.

The plan says --class replaces --reason (making reason empty for a normal --class close), but the plan's Files list for transition_close.py only says 'thread close_class through the close path', not 'update the bug_close_reason_ok guard in _completion_precheck'.
findings.finding: bug_close_reason_ok has two call sites ‚Äî txn.transition_core (line 228) AND _completion_precheck in transition_close.py (line 121) ‚Äî but the plan only names txn.py for the validation replacement. If --class replaces --reason (making reason empty), _completion_precheck's existing check `if ticket_type == 'bug' and not txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason)` will raise a CommandError blocking every completion-verified bug close, even with a valid --class value.
scenarios: A user runs `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --class regression` with the completion-verification gate enabled; _completion_precheck fires, sees reason='' and ticket_type='bug', calls bug_close_reason_ok('') which returns False, and raises 'closing a bug requires --reason starting with Fixed: or Escalated to user:' ‚Äî the close is blocked even though --class regression was supplied.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` to the Files list and specify a fold rule in `process_status` (analogous to `_fold_claimed_session`) that promotes `data.get('close_class')` into `state['close_class']` on the `* ‚Üí closed` STATUS edge.
evidence: Tool read of _processors.py lines 171‚Äì192: _fold_claimed_session only promotes 'session', 'harness', 'remote_session' from status_data; no generic passthrough of extra keys exists.
AC: 'rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e \''.close_class=="regression"'\'' ‚Äî requires close_class to appear in reduced ticket state.
Plan Files list: _processors.py is not named; only transition.py, txn.py, transition_close.py, ticket_state.schema.json, and docs/tests are listed.
Tool search for 'close_class' in src/rebar/reducer returned zero matches.
findings.finding: The reducer's STATUS processor (`_processors.py` / `_fold_claimed_session`) only promotes specific status_data keys into ticket state; `close_class` written into `status_data` will NOT appear in `rebar show` output without an explicit fold rule ‚Äî but `_processors.py` is absent from the plan's Files list.
scenarios: Executor writes close_class into status_data in txn.py (as planned), but the reducer never folds it into state[], so 'rebar show' JSON omits it and the primary AC jq assertion fails.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the `bug_close_reason_ok(reason)` guard in `transition_close.py:_completion_precheck` (line 121) is updated or removed as part of this change ‚Äî it is a second call site of the predicate being replaced.
evidence: Tool search for 'bug_close_reason_ok': matched transition_close.py:121 (`if ticket_type == 'bug' and not txn.bug_close_reason_ok(reason)`) in addition to txn.py:150 and txn.py:228.
Plan Approach: 'validate the enum for bug closes (replacing bug_close_reason_ok)' ‚Äî names only txn.py as the replacement site.
Plan Files: transition_close.py is listed only for 'thread close_class through the close path', not for updating the reason guard.
findings.finding: The plan's replacement of `bug_close_reason_ok` is specified only for `txn.py`, but the same predicate is also called in `transition_close.py` (line 121, inside `_completion_precheck`); the plan does not say how that second call site is handled.
findings.location: Files list / src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py line 121 / Approach section
scenarios: After the change, transition_close.py still calls bug_close_reason_ok(reason) on a bug close with --class regression (no --reason); the completion precheck raises 'Error: closing a bug requires --reason...' before txn.transition_core even runs, blocking the close even though --class is now the intended signal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (and corresponding test case) specifying what happens when `--class` is omitted on a bug close: either (a) required ‚Üí exit nonzero with the allowed values listed, or (b) silently defaults to `undetermined`.
evidence: Plan Approach: '`--class` param ‚Ä¶ replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`' ‚Äî implies required, but does not state it.
Plan AC: tests only '--class regression' (exit 0), invalid class (exit !=0), and '--class undetermined' (exit 0); no AC covers a bug close with no --class flag.

Today bug_close_reason_ok enforces --reason as required (txn.py:221-232: 'if not close_reason: raise CommandError'). The plan says 'replacing' it, but does not say 'required' or 'defaults to undetermined'.
The additive/optional field note ('readers tolerate absence') describes the schema field, not the CLI requirement ‚Äî these are separate questions.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the fallback behavior when `--class` is omitted on a bug close ‚Äî whether it is required (non-zero exit), optional with a default (e.g. `undetermined`), or simply allowed to be absent ‚Äî leaving the processing rule ambiguous for implementers and the AC incomplete.
scenarios: An executor who reads 'optional field' makes --class optional with no default, silently writing no close_class; another reads 'replacing --reason' and makes it required, breaking backward compat for existing scripts that pass --reason but not --class.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and add an AC for whether `--class` is required or optional when closing a bug, and test the omitted-`--class` path explicitly (error with message, or silent omission with documented intent).
evidence: 'optional field (readers tolerate absence)' implies optional
'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text --reason' implies required (since --reason was required)
No AC covers `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` (no --class) ‚Äî success or error?
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `--class` is required or optional when closing a bug. The schema marks `close_class` as optional, yet the plan says it 'replaces' the formerly required `--reason`, implying it may be mandatory. No AC covers the omitted-`--class` case, leaving the user-facing behavior undefined.
scenarios: User omits --class on a bug close; unclear whether they get a validation error or a silent omission of close_class
Metric consumers see tickets with no close_class, ambiguously indistinguishable from pre-feature tickets
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` (no --class) either exits nonzero with a message listing allowed values, OR exits 0 with `close_class` absent and document that omission is intentional. Pick one and encode it in the test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly defines and tests the behavior when `--class` is omitted from a bug close (either: 'exits 0 and `close_class` is absent/`undetermined`' or 'exits nonzero with a message naming --class as required').
evidence: ACs test --class regression (happy path), invalid class (rejection), and --class undetermined, but contain no AC for closing a bug without --class at all.
Plan says 'optional field (readers tolerate absence)' for the schema, but the CLI behavior on omission is unspecified ‚Äî it could default to 'undetermined', error, or silently omit the field.
Absence of any AC line like: 'closing a bug without --class [succeeds with default X | is rejected with message Y]'.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether --class is required or optional for a bug close at the CLI level, and neither branch (omission-is-an-error OR omission-silently-defaults) is covered by a test, leaving the most common user mistake (closing a bug without --class) untested.
scenarios: User runs `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed` (omitting --class entirely) ‚Äî behavior is undefined and untested; could silently drop the field or raise an unhelpful error.
If --class is secretly required, the omission path is a failure path on a new user-facing flow with no test coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one AC line: 'closing a bug without `--class` [succeeds, writing no `close_class` field | defaults to `undetermined` | is rejected with exit != 0 and a message listing --class]' ‚Äî pick one, then add a CLI subprocess assertion for it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicitly note non-applicability) verifying the removal side of the migration: e.g., assert that omitting `--reason` no longer causes a nonzero exit, or that `--reason` is rejected with a clear message if it has been removed.
evidence: Approach: 'replacing `bug_close_reason_ok`' and 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`'
ACs only assert: close_class written, invalid class rejected, undetermined accepted ‚Äî none assert `--reason` is gone or no longer required
findings.finding: The plan describes replacing `--reason` (a migration), but no AC verifies the removal/deprecation side ‚Äî only the replacement (`close_class` persistence) is tested. For a migration, criteria should verify BOTH sides.
scenarios: If `--reason` validation code is left in place, the migration is incomplete but all ACs still pass

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether `--reason` is removed, deprecated-with-warning, or kept alongside `--class`, and add an AC (or explicit deferral note) covering the `--reason` backward-compatibility story.
evidence: Plan title: 'Persist a bug close --class enum (replacing the discarded reason)'
Approach: 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`'
Files bullet: 'parse a new `--class <value>` flag in `_parse_flags` (alongside --reason)'
No AC tests the fate of `--reason` (removed? still accepted? error?)
findings.finding: The title and Approach say `--reason` is being 'replaced' by `--class`, but the file-level description for `transition.py` says the new flag is added 'alongside `--reason`', leaving it ambiguous whether `--reason` is removed, deprecated-but-kept, or silently ignored after this change. Callers currently passing `--reason` on bug closes are affected differently depending on which interpretation is correct, and no AC covers the fate of `--reason`.
findings.location: Files section ‚Äî `transition.py` bullet: 'parse a new `--class <value>` flag in `_parse_flags` (alongside --reason)'
scenarios: A caller invoking `rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --reason 'fixed upstream'` today: if `--reason` is silently dropped, the call succeeds with no signal; if it becomes an error, existing scripts break ‚Äî neither outcome is specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Approach: 'The existing `--reason` flag will be removed (or deprecated with a warning) as part of this change; any caller passing `--reason` on a bug close will receive [error / deprecation warning].' Then add an AC: '`rebar transition <bug> in_progress closed --reason old_value` exits nonzero (or with a deprecation warning) after this change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and corresponding CLI subprocess test) specifying what happens when `--reason` is passed after this change ‚Äî e.g., 'passing `--reason <text>` to a bug close exits nonzero with an 'unknown flag' message' ‚Äî to guard the deleted behavior.
evidence: Plan states: 'Persist a bounded bug-classification enum at close, replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text --reason.'
Plan states --reason is 'required today' and `bug_close_reason_ok` is being replaced.
No AC addresses what happens when a user (or existing script) passes `--reason` after this change ships.
findings.finding: The plan replaces/removes the previously-required `--reason` flag but includes no test asserting that `--reason` is no longer accepted (or is explicitly handled), leaving a changed/deleted behavior uncovered.
scenarios: Existing automation or documented workflows pass `--reason <text>`; after this change they either silently ignore it or break with an unhelpful 'unknown flag' error ‚Äî neither outcome is tested.
If --reason is kept as a deprecated alias, that behavior is also untested.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact JSON path of `close_class` in `rebar show --output json` output and update AC1's jq expression to match (e.g., `.close_class` if merged to top-level, or `.status_data.close_class` if nested).
evidence: Plan stores `close_class` in `status_data` (txn.py writes 'close_class into status_data')
Schema addition is to `ticket_state.schema.json` as an optional field, suggesting it may be top-level on the ticket state ‚Äî but the nesting is unspecified

If `rebar show` surfaces the latest STATUS event's `status_data` merged into the ticket object, the path is `.close_class`; if nested, the path is `.status_data.close_class` or `.events[-1].status_data.close_class`
findings.finding: AC1's jq assertion `jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'` assumes `close_class` is a top-level key in `rebar show <bug> --output json`, but the plan stores it inside `status_data` of the STATUS event. The plan does not specify the JSON shape of `rebar show --output json`, so the correct jq path is unknowable from the plan text.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria ‚Äî AC1: `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.close_class=="regression"'`
scenarios: Executor correctly implements the feature, but `close_class` is at `.status_data.close_class` in show output ‚Äî AC1's jq exits nonzero, falsely failing the criterion
Or the path is correct but undocumented, leaving future maintainers unable to trust the proving command
findings.suggested_fix: If `ticket_state.schema.json` surfaces `close_class` at the top level of the ticket document, keep `.close_class`. If stored under `status_data`, change to: `rebar show <bug> --output json | jq -e '.status_data.close_class=="regression"'`. Add one line to the plan noting the expected output shape.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve and document the disposition of `--reason`: clarify in the plan (and in the listed doc targets) whether `--reason` is removed, deprecated-with-warning, or kept alongside `--class`, and ensure no doc/help text is left referencing a behavior that no longer holds.
evidence: 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`' (What section)
'parse a new `--class <value>` flag in _parse_flags (alongside --reason)' (Files section)
Doc targets listed: transition.txt, ticket-model.md, user-guide.md ‚Äî none explicitly tasked with documenting the --reason disposition
findings.finding: The plan contradicts itself on the fate of `--reason`: the What section says `--class` is 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`', but the Files section says transition.py will parse `--class` 'alongside --reason'. If `--reason` is actually removed or deprecated, any existing help text, docs, or user-guide prose referencing `--reason` becomes stale; if it is kept, the three doc targets need to explicitly clarify the relationship between `--class` and `--reason` so users aren't confused.
scenarios: If --reason is removed: users who have scripts using --reason get no deprecation notice; existing user-guide prose referencing --reason becomes stale.
If --reason is kept: users don't know whether --reason is still required, optional, or ignored alongside --class, creating confusion.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to the Approach section: e.g. '`--reason` is retained for backward compatibility but its value is ignored; a future cleanup ticket will remove it' OR '`--reason` is removed in this change ‚Äî transition.txt and user-guide.md must strike all references to it.' Then task one of the listed doc files explicitly with covering the transition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction: state explicitly whether `--reason` is *removed* from the CLI, *deprecated-but-accepted*, or *kept alongside* `--class`, and update both the Files and Approach sections to match.
evidence: Files: 'parse a new `--class <value>` flag in `_parse_flags` (alongside `--reason`)'
What: 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text `--reason`'
Approach: 'replacing it with a bounded enum'
findings.finding: The Files section says parse `--class` 'alongside `--reason`', while the What and Approach sections say `--class` is 'replacing' `--reason`. This sends the implementer in two directions: keep `--reason` as a live (if vestigial) flag, or remove it.
findings.location: Files section ('alongside --reason') vs What / Approach sections ('replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text --reason')
scenarios: Implementer keeps `--reason` as a dead but accepted flag when the intent was removal, leaving tech debt
Implementer removes `--reason` when the intent was coexistence, breaking scripts that pass `--reason`
findings.suggested_fix: If removing: change Files wording to 'replacing `--reason`' and add an AC asserting `--reason` is no longer accepted (or at least no longer required). If keeping alongside: change Approach wording to 'supplementing' and note the coexistence policy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a formal `close_class` enum $def in `common.schema.json` (listing all 9 values) and `$ref` it from the new `close_class` property in `ticket_state.schema.json`, matching the pattern used by `ticket_type` and `relation`.
evidence: Plan Approach: 'Closed MECE vocabulary with an escape + hygiene values: regression, plan_defect, env_integration, flaky, preexisting, not_a_bug, duplicate, escalated, undetermined' ‚Äî defined only in prose.
Tool search for 'close_class' across src/rebar returned zero matches.
common.schema.json carries all other bounded enums (ticket_status, ticket_type, relation, creation_channel) as formal enum:[...] $defs; no close_class $def exists.
ticket_state.schema.json has no close_class property at all (confirmed by read_file).
Plan says 'add optional close_class' to ticket_state.schema.json but does not specify whether the enum constraint lives there inline or as a $ref to common.schema.json.
findings.finding: The close_class enum vocabulary is defined only in plan prose; no schema $defs location is specified, and the field is absent from both ticket_state.schema.json and common.schema.json today.
scenarios: An executor adding the field to ticket_state.schema.json as a plain string (no enum constraint) passes schema validation but allows any value ‚Äî defeating the bounded-enum goal.
A validator that reads common.schema.json for the enum (as with ticket_type/relation) finds nothing and accepts or rejects all values inconsistently.
findings.suggested_fix: In common.schema.json add: `"close_class": {"type": "string", "enum": ["regression", "plan_defect", "env_integration", "flaky", "preexisting", "not_a_bug", "duplicate", "escalated", "undetermined"]}`. In ticket_state.schema.json add: `"close_class": {"$ref": "common.schema.json#/$defs/close_class"}` under properties.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide an explicit rollback/migration path for the `--reason` removal: either (a) describe an expand-contract sequence (accept both flags for one release, then drop `--reason`), or (b) document that `--reason` was never a public/stable interface and callers are enumerated/internal-only, justifying a hard cut.
evidence: Approach: 'back-out = stop requiring/writing it' (refers to close_class, not to restoring --reason)
What: 'replacing today's validated-then-discarded free-text --reason' implies --reason is no longer accepted
No expand-contract or deprecation phase mentioned for --reason removal
findings.finding: The plan removes `--reason` as a required CLI input (a breaking change for any caller currently passing `--reason`), but the stated rollback ‚Äî 'back-out = stop requiring/writing it' ‚Äî addresses only the new `close_class` field, not the restoration of the old `--reason` contract. No expand-contract sequence is described for the `--reason` removal.
scenarios: CI pipelines or documented workflows that currently pass `--reason` break immediately on deploy with no migration window
A rollback of close_class does not restore the old --reason requirement, leaving callers with neither interface
findings.suggested_fix: If `--reason` is internal-only: add a sentence to Approach stating 'all callers are internal; no external migration needed'. If external callers exist: add a deprecation phase where `--reason` is accepted but ignored with a warning before removal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and test the behavior of `--class` on non-bug ticket closes: either reject with a clear error (and add an AC for it) or document that it is silently ignored.
evidence: 'parse a new --class <value> flag in _parse_flags (alongside --reason)' ‚Äî flag is parsed at the transition layer, not scoped to bug tickets
No AC or test covers non-bug ticket types with --class supplied
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what happens when `--class` is supplied on a non-bug ticket close (e.g., closing a task or story). The flag is parsed in `transition.py` generically, so it may silently be accepted and persisted, or produce a confusing error.
scenarios: User runs `rebar transition <task> open closed --class regression`; flag is silently accepted (confusing) or rejected with no documented error message

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a single source of truth for the `close_class` vocabulary (e.g., a constant tuple/list in a shared module, or derive CLI validation from the JSON schema's `enum` array) and reference it from transition.py, txn.py, and test fixtures so a vocabulary change is a one-file edit.
evidence: Vocabulary listed inline in Approach: 'regression, plan_defect, env_integration, flaky, preexisting, not_a_bug, duplicate, escalated, undetermined'
Three separate files (transition.py, txn.py, schema.json) all need to know the allowed values ‚Äî no shared constant or schema-driven validation mentioned
Plan notes 'Closed MECE vocabulary' implying intentional design, but makes no mention of centralisation
findings.finding: The nine-value enum vocabulary (`regression`, `plan_defect`, `env_integration`, ‚Ä¶) is presumably defined independently in at least transition.py (flag validation / help text), txn.py (enum guard), and ticket_state.schema.json (allowed values). The plan does not designate a single source of truth. When a new class value needs to be added, the author must discover and update every definition site, creating a silent inconsistency risk.
findings.location: ## Approach (vocabulary list) and ## Files (transition.py, txn.py, ticket_state.schema.json bullets)
scenarios: A future contributor adds a new class value to the schema but forgets to update the CLI validator in transition.py ‚Äî the value passes schema validation but is rejected at the CLI.
txn.py and transition.py independently enumerate the values and diverge over time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` to the Files edit-set: add `close_class: str = ''` to the `transition()` signature and forward it to `transition_compute`.
evidence: ABSENCE: `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` not listed in Files section
`_lib_writes.py:transition()` (lines 329-402) accepts `reason=str` and forwards to `transition_compute`; the MCP `transition_ticket` tool calls `rebar.transition(...)` which delegates here
Plan says `--class` is a new required-for-bugs parameter that threads through `transition_compute` and `close_ticket`
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py` (`transition()` library function) is an unenumerated consumer: it is the Python-API entry point for transitions and currently accepts `reason=str` which it forwards to `transition_compute`; it has no `close_class` parameter, so library callers (non-CLI users) cannot supply the new required field.
scenarios: A library caller (or MCP agent) closing a bug ticket via `rebar.transition(id, 'in_progress', 'closed')` cannot pass `close_class`, so bug-close validation in txn.py rejects it or silently writes no class.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` as a consumer to update: either pass `close_class='undetermined'` as a safe default for imported bug closes, or document the chosen bypass strategy.
evidence: ABSENCE: `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` not listed in Files section
`import_ndjson.py:356`: `transition_compute(local, 'open', 'closed', force=True, repo_root=rr)` with no reason/class ‚Äî used for every closed ticket in an import payload, including bugs
Plan says the enum is required for bug closes (replacing the existing required `--reason`)
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` is an unenumerated consumer that calls `transition_compute(local, 'open', 'closed', force=True, repo_root=rr)` for imported closed bug tickets without supplying any `reason` or `close_class`; if `close_class` becomes required (or even just validated) for bug closes, this import path will break.
findings.location: Files section / Approach section ('replacing it with a bounded enum')
scenarios: Running `rebar import` on an NDJSON payload containing closed bug tickets fails mid-import (or silently leaves bugs with no `close_class`), breaking the import contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass `close_class='undetermined'` (the plan's designated escape value) when calling `transition_compute` for closed bugs during import.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific consumers of `close_class` (dashboards, metric queries, report scripts) in the plan and confirm each handles field absence explicitly (e.g., `coalesce`, null-check, or schema version guard).
evidence: 'the vocabulary that feeds the classification metrics' ‚Äî consumers unnamed
'additive/optional field (readers tolerate absence)' ‚Äî asserts tolerance but names no specific reader to verify
findings.finding: The plan mentions 'classification metrics' as the consumer of `close_class` but does not enumerate which metrics, pipelines, or readers will consume this field, so it is impossible to verify that all consumers tolerate its absence on pre-feature tickets or on non-bug closes.
findings.location: ## Approach ('the vocabulary that feeds the classification metrics') / src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json
scenarios: A dashboard or reporting query assumes close_class is always present on closed bugs, throwing null-reference errors on historical tickets
A new metric reader is added post-launch without knowing which tickets pre-date the field

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/types.py` to the Files edit-set and add `close_class: NotRequired[str | None]` to the `TicketState` TypedDict.
evidence: ABSENCE: `src/rebar/types.py` not listed in Files section
`types.py:TicketState` TypedDict (lines 85‚Äì118) mirrors `ticket_state.schema.json` property-for-property (e.g. `claimed_session`, `last_reopened_at`, `creation_channel` all appear in both)
All recent schema additions (e.g. `claimed_session`, `creation_channel`) have corresponding `NotRequired` entries in `TicketState`
findings.finding: `src/rebar/types.py` (`TicketState` TypedDict) is an unenumerated edit target: it is the canonical typed shape for `rebar show` / `show_ticket` return values and must gain `close_class: NotRequired[str | None]` when `ticket_state.schema.json` adds the field.
scenarios: Typed library callers using `TicketState` get no type-safety for `close_class`; static analysis (mypy/pyright) won't recognize the new field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-rollback schema-state assertion to the Approach section: state that already-persisted `close_class` values in STATUS events are tolerated after rollback because `ticket_state.schema.json` already carries `additionalProperties: true`, and note that any future tightening of that flag must account for these orphaned fields.
evidence: Plan quote: 'back-out = stop requiring/writing it'
The criterion states: 'the plan must assert on the schema STATE AFTER rollback, not merely that rollback exits 0'
Already-persisted STATUS events carrying `close_class` remain in the append-only store permanently after rollback; the plan does not state whether readers tolerate these orphaned values

The existing `ticket_state.schema.json` already has `additionalProperties: true` (line 7 of the file), which means orphaned values ARE tolerated ‚Äî but the plan never cites this, leaving the post-rollback state un-asserted
findings.finding: The plan's rollback description ('back-out = stop requiring/writing it') only addresses the write-side; it does not assert the schema STATE of already-persisted `close_class` events after rollback ‚Äî the criterion requires an explicit assertion, not just that rollback exits 0.
scenarios: A team rolls back by removing the `--class` flag from the CLI and stops writing `close_class`; existing events with `close_class` stay in the store. If a future reader or validator ever tightens `additionalProperties: false`, those events break.

A partial rollout (some instances on old code, some on new) reads events with `close_class`; without the explicit assertion reviewers can't confirm whether old-code readers silently ignore or error on the field.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, extend the back-out statement to: 'Back-out = stop requiring/writing it; events already carrying `close_class` remain valid because `ticket_state.schema.json` is `additionalProperties: true` ‚Äî readers on any version tolerate the orphaned field. Any future tightening of `additionalProperties` must either drop `close_class` via a compaction sweep first or keep the explicit allow-list entry.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
